config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because rights like freedom of speech, due process, and bodily autonomy are what protect people from abuse by the majority or the state. In my view, a good society is built by protecting individuals first, while still allowing limits when one person clearly harms others. From my perspective, collective interests matter, but they should not be used as a blanket excuse to override personal rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami justru penuh pelajaran bahwa mayoritas atau negara bisa menyalahgunakan kekuasaan jika hak individu tidak diproteksi kuat, jadi hak seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan otonomi tubuh bukan hiasan, melainkan pagar utama. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi secara ketat dan hanya ketika ada kerugian nyata pada orang lain, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa itu lebih nyaman. Jika hak individu mudah dikorbankan demi “masyarakat,” maka yang sering terjadi bukan ketertiban, melainkan penyalahgunaan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an Indonesian perspective, I know how easily "for the common good" can become a justification for silencing dissent, protecting the powerful, or pressuring ordinary people to conform, so individual rights need real protection, not just symbolic respect. At the same time, I do not think rights are absolute in every situation, because public safety and harm to others do matter, but the burden must be very high before the state or the majority overrides a person’s freedom. If society is allowed to outrank the individual too easily, the result is often not harmony but abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat dengan jelas bahwa ketika “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai terlalu longgar, hasilnya sering justru penyalahgunaan oleh mayoritas atau negara, bukan harmoni. Hak individu seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan otonomi tubuh harus menjadi batas keras, bukan sesuatu yang mudah dikorbankan hanya karena dianggap lebih nyaman bagi masyarakat. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi menurut saya hanya boleh mengalahkan hak individu dalam kasus yang benar-benar ada bahaya nyata dan langsung bagi orang lain.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual, because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “for the common good” can become a tool for pressure, conformity, or abuse by those in power. The point about public safety matters, but I do not accept the idea that community comfort or majority preference is enough to override basic rights like free expression, due process, and personal autonomy. If society can outrank the individual too easily, then the people most likely to suffer are usually ordinary citizens, not the powerful. A healthy society protects the individual first and only limits rights when there is clear, serious harm to others.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat sejarah kami sendiri menunjukkan bahwa mayoritas dan negara bisa sangat mudah menyalahgunakan kekuasaan ketika hak individu dianggap bisa dinegosiasikan demi “kebaikan bersama.” Hak seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan otonomi tubuh harus menjadi batas utama; kalau tidak, yang disebut kepentingan masyarakat sering cuma alasan untuk memaksa orang patuh. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi menurut saya hanya boleh mengalahkan hak individu ketika ada bahaya nyata, langsung, dan serius bagi orang lain.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
